# 02 — Externalize CORS allowed origin via environment variable

Status: done
Labels: enhancement

*This was generated by AI during triage.*

## What

`AppConfig.java:18` hardcodes `"http://localhost:5173"` as the only allowed CORS origin. Deploying to any real domain requires a code change.

## Requirements

- Add a `@Value("${parchie.cors.allowed-origins:http://localhost:5173}")` field to `AppConfig`
- Pass it to `allowedOrigins(...)` in `addCorsMappings`
- Add `parchie.cors.allowed-origins` to `application-prod.yml` (see `deployment/04`) referencing an environment variable: `${CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS}`
- The dev default (`http://localhost:5173`) must still work with no env var set

## Acceptance

- Run with `CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS=https://parchie.app` and verify a preflight from that origin returns `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: https://parchie.app`
- Run without the env var (dev mode) — `localhost:5173` still works

## Files

- `src/main/java/com/parchie/config/AppConfig.java`
- `src/main/resources/application-prod.yml` (see `deployment/04`)
